location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

seniority.level: Staff / Group PM (people manager, IC6+ equivalent)
level_signals: 6+ years of experience in product management
2+ years of experience managing product managers
Leads a large cross-functional team and manages a team of PMs
Comp range $176K-$220K base, consistent with senior people-manager PM at Lyft

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of experience in product management, product design or equivalent consultant experience with significant time spent leading consumer mobile apps
2+ years of experience managing product managers
significant time spent leading consumer mobile apps
Passion for Lyft and what we are trying to achieve in sustainable urban transportation

Natural ability to make things happen around you. You manage project ambiguity, complexity and interdependencies in an organized and structured way. Be able to define and analyze metrics that inform the success of products
Excellent judgment and thoughtfulness about the dynamics of a product ecosystem
Strong communication skills and intuition for communication strategy at every level -- within teams, within the company, to users, to press, and other audiences
Experience leading post-launch optimizations including developing testing strategies
Ability to conceptualize, manage and prioritize multiple projects for stakeholders
Detail oriented with superior organizational skills -- balancing multiple projects, deadlines, and requests should be second nature to you

scope.team_size_estimate: unclear (manages a team of PMs + large cross-functional team)
scope.surface: Lyft consumer app — home screen, request flow, pickup experience, in-ride experience, rate & pay; AV extensions; segments including Lyft Silver for Seniors and Price Lock for commuters
primary_metrics_inferred: Rider conversion and completion rate through request flow
Pickup and in-trip experience quality (ratings, reliability)
Revenue metrics tied to Price Lock and premium experiences
core_responsibilities: Understand Lyft's strategic and competitive position; set team goals and success metrics
Set an ambitious vision that scales across places and situations of all types
Own team's products from ideation through development, experiment, launch, growth and maintenance
Lead and align cross functional partners and teams to drive a shared vision
Communicate clear roadmaps, priorities, experiments and decisions across audiences from partner teams to executive level

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($176K-$220K base) plus equity and bonus mentioned
Clear product surface with specific ownership areas named (home screen, request flow, in-ride, rate & pay, AV, Silver, Price Lock)
High-impact, high-visibility role touching millions of daily active users
Hybrid schedule with defined in-office days (Mon/Wed/Thu) and flexibility to work remotely up to 4 weeks per year
Comprehensive benefits package including 18 weeks paid parental leave
red_flags: Hybrid role requires in-office 3 days per week (Mon, Wed, Thu) in San Francisco — eliminates remote candidates outside SF
Visa sponsorship not mentioned; unclear for international applicants
Role combines IC strategic depth with people management of PMs and large XFN teams — broad scope may stretch capacity
No explicit mention of preferred or bonus qualifications — hard to gauge where bar is set for competitive candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, people management requirement, and cross-functional scope)
scope.team_size_estimate (not stated; described as 'team of PMs' and 'large cross-functional team')
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
ambiguities: JD lists no 'Preferred' or 'Nice to Have' section; all Experience bullets treated as must-haves with hard severity. No nice-to-haves were separable.
Years experience requirement covers 'product management, product design or equivalent consultant experience' — whether design-only backgrounds qualify is ambiguous.
